question: What is the exact value of $\sin(60^\circ)$?
Solve for $x \in [0^\circ; 180^\circ]$: $2\cos^2(x) - \cos(x) - 1 = 0$.
explanation: Let $k = \cos(x)$. $2k^2 - k - 1 = 0 \implies (2k + 1)(k - 1) = 0$. So $\cos(x) = -1/2$ or $\cos(x) = 1$. In $[0^\circ; 180^\circ]$, $x = 120^\circ$ or $x = 0^\circ$.

In $\Delta ABC$, $a = 13$, $\angle B = 33^\circ$, and $\angle C = 53^\circ$. Calculate the area of the triangle (to 1 decimal place).
explanation: $\angle A = 180^\circ - (33^\circ + 53^\circ) = 94^\circ$. By Sine Rule, $c = \frac{13\sin 53^\circ}{\sin 94^\circ} \approx 10.41$. Area = $\frac{1}{2}ac\sin B \approx 36.8$.

question: In $\Delta ABC$, $a = 10$, $\angle B = 56^\circ$, and $\angle C = 40^\circ$. Calculate the area of the triangle (to 1 decimal place).
explanation: $\angle A = 180^\circ - (56^\circ + 40^\circ) = 84^\circ$. By Sine Rule, $c = \frac{10\sin 40^\circ}{\sin 84^\circ} \approx 6.46$. Area = $\frac{1}{2}ac\sin B \approx 26.8$.

question: In $\Delta ABC$, $a = 15$, $\angle B = 49^\circ$, and $\angle C = 62^\circ$. Calculate the area of the triangle (to 1 decimal place).
explanation: $\angle A = 180^\circ - (49^\circ + 62^\circ) = 69^\circ$. By Sine Rule, $c = \frac{15\sin 62^\circ}{\sin 69^\circ} \approx 14.19$. Area = $\frac{1}{2}ac\sin B \approx 80.3$.

question: In $\Delta ABC$, $a = 16$, $\angle B = 58^\circ$, and $\angle C = 42^\circ$. Calculate the area of the triangle (to 1 decimal place).
explanation: $\angle A = 180^\circ - (58^\circ + 42^\circ) = 80^\circ$. By Sine Rule, $c = \frac{16\sin 42^\circ}{\sin 80^\circ} \approx 10.87$. Area = $\frac{1}{2}ac\sin B \approx 73.8$.

question: Solve for $\theta \in [0^\circ; 90^\circ]$: $\sin(\theta) = 0.74$ (correct to 1 decimal place).
In $\Delta ABC$, $a = 20$, $\angle B = 39^\circ$, and $\angle C = 72^\circ$. Calculate the area of the triangle (to 1 decimal place).
explanation: $\angle A = 180^\circ - (39^\circ + 72^\circ) = 69^\circ$. By Sine Rule, $c = \frac{20\sin 72^\circ}{\sin 69^\circ} \approx 20.37$. Area = $\frac{1}{2}ac\sin B \approx 128.2$.

question: In $\Delta ABC$, $a = 11$, $\angle B = 33^\circ$, and $\angle C = 37^\circ$. Calculate the area of the triangle (to 1 decimal place).
explanation: $\angle A = 180^\circ - (33^\circ + 37^\circ) = 110^\circ$. By Sine Rule, $c = \frac{11\sin 37^\circ}{\sin 110^\circ} \approx 7.04$. Area = $\frac{1}{2}ac\sin B \approx 21.1$.

question: In $\Delta ABC$, $a = 13$, $\angle B = 56^\circ$, and $\angle C = 63^\circ$. Calculate the area of the triangle (to 1 decimal place).
explanation: $\angle A = 180^\circ - (56^\circ + 63^\circ) = 61^\circ$. By Sine Rule, $c = \frac{13\sin 63^\circ}{\sin 61^\circ} \approx 13.24$. Area = $\frac{1}{2}ac\sin B \approx 71.4$.

question: In $\Delta ABC$, $a = 18$, $\angle B = 58^\circ$, and $\angle C = 64^\circ$. Calculate the area of the triangle (to 1 decimal place).
explanation: $\angle A = 180^\circ - (58^\circ + 64^\circ) = 58^\circ$. By Sine Rule, $c = \frac{18\sin 64^\circ}{\sin 58^\circ} \approx 19.08$. Area = $\frac{1}{2}ac\sin B \approx 145.6$.

svg.content:
<svg viewBox="0 0 200 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="100%" height="200px" style="max-width: 300px; margin: auto; display: block;">
  <polygon points="100,40 160,140 40,140" fill="none" stroke="#333333" stroke-width="3"/>
  <text x="100" y="160" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333333" font-family="sans-serif">a=18</text>
  <text x="50" y="90" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333333" font-family="sans-serif">B=58°</text>
  <text x="150" y="90" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333333" font-family="sans-serif">C=64°</text>
</svg>

</svg>
